perturbation.original_text: 3. For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital", "Company Action Level RBC", and "Surplus to Policyholders" shall have the meanings ascribed thereto under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State, or, with respect to "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC", if not defined therein, shall have the meanings ascribed thereto in the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC"). The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be equal to the percentage set forth on Schedule 1 attached hereto, which shall be agreed to by AIG and the Company at least once every year beginning upon the date of the filing of the Company's 2010 Annual Statement with the Domiciliary State's insurance department and following review against the capital adequacy standards and criteria ("Agency Criteria") of each of Standard & Poor's Corp. ("S&P"), Moody's Investors Service ("Moody's") and A.M. Best Company ("A.M. Best"). Notwithstanding the obligation of the Company and AIG to review the Specified Minimum Percentage on an annual basis, the parties hereto agree to review and revise the Specified Minimum Percentage on a more frequent basis, if the parties agree it is appropriate, to take into account (a) any material changes after the date hereof to any Agency Criteria adopted by any of S&P, Moody's or A.M. Best, on the one hand, or to the law of the Domiciliary State or NAIC RBC rules or instructions, on the other hand, which causes the results under the Agency Criteria to diverge from that under the law of the Domiciliary State or NAIC RBC rules or instructions, (b) the Company completes a material transaction that is treated materially differently by the Agency Criteria, on the one hand, and the NAIC RBC rules or instructions, on the other hand, or (c) any other material development or circumstance affecting the Company which AIG and the Company agree merits a reevaluation of the Specified Minimum Percentage then in effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital", "Company Action Level RBC", and "Surplus to Policyholders" shall have the meanings ascribed thereto under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State, or, with respect to "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC", if not defined therein, shall have the meanings ascribed thereto in the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC"). The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be determined by AIG. Notwithstanding the obligation of the Company and AIG to review the Specified Minimum Percentage on an annual basis, the parties hereto agree to review and revise the Specified Minimum Percentage on a more frequent basis, if the parties agree it is appropriate, to take into account (a) any material changes after the date hereof to any Agency Criteria adopted by any of S&P, Moody's or A.M. Best, on the one hand, or to the law of the Domiciliary State or NAIC RBC rules or instructions, on the other hand, which causes the results under the Agency Criteria to diverge from that under the law of the Domiciliary State or NAIC RBC rules or instructions, (b) the Company completes a material transaction that is treated materially differently by the Agency Criteria, on the one hand, and the NAIC RBC rules or instructions, on the other hand, or (c) any other material development or circumstance affecting the Company which AIG and the Company agree merits a reevaluation of the Specified Minimum Percentage then in effect.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of how the "Specified Minimum Percentage" is determined and removing the link to Schedule 1, and making it soley defined by AIG, while retaining language about reviewing it against Agency Criteria. The definition creates an internal contradiction because the review against specific criteria suggests a collaborative or objective standard, but the sole determination by AIG implies complete unilateral control, contradicting the notion of a review process using objective standards outlined later in the paragraph. The contradiction directly impacts the enforceability of clauses relying on this percentage. The old schedule also had a percentage defined, changing it to be determined by AIG will contradict the final 'SCHEDULE 1' which mentions that `The Specified Minimum Percentage shall initially equal 350% of the Company's Company Action Level RBC.` This percentage will not be valid anymore, since AIG will determine its value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SCHEDULE 1 The Specified Minimum Percentage shall initially equal 350% of the Company's Company Action Level RBC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives AIG the sole power to determine the 'Specified Minimum Percentage', while the contradicted text (SCHEDULE 1) states that 'The Specified Minimum Percentage shall initially equal 350% of the Company's Company Action Level RBC.' The modification directly contradicts the previously defined initial value of the percentage, creating an irreconcilable conflict between the two clauses. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the limitations on AIG's right to terminate the agreement, AIG has now unlimited ability to terminate, while Section 9 states that `Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement` By removing the conditions when AIG can terminate, this contradicts Section 9, because the policy holder's protection is limited to after the termination of the agreement, so if it's at AIG's will, it would contradict it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this 5 Agreement; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that no policyholder of the Company may take any action authorized under this paragraph 9 unless and until (a) such policyholder has given AIG written notice of its intent to enforce the terms of this Agreement as provided in this paragraph 9, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of and basis for the policyholder's complaint and (b) AIG has failed to comply with this Agreement within sixty (60) days after such notice is given; and, PROVIDED, FURTHER, that upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with paragraph 8 hereof, the rights of any policyholder as provided for under this paragraph 9 shall terminate effective as of the Termination Date, except with respect to the obligation of AIG (if any) to make capital contributions to the Company pursuant to paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement solely to the extent such obligation arose prior to, and remained unsatisfied as of, the Termination Date (it being understood that upon AIG's satisfaction of all such obligations after the Termination Date, no such policyholder shall have any rights against the Company or AIG, as the case may be, under this paragraph 9).
perturbation.justification: The original text limited AIG's ability to terminate the agreement. The changed text removes these limitations, granting AIG an unlimited right to terminate. Section 9 grants policyholders rights contingent on the agreement's existence prior to termination. With AIG's unlimited termination rights, these policyholder rights become significantly undermined, because AIG can simply terminate the agreement at any time, contradicting the intent and enforceability of Section 9. Therefore, a clear contradiction exists.
